event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: Pinned black-box evidence exposes only timedata role/framing, opaque identifier echo, four timestamp roles, bounded exchange count behavior, provenance hashes, and strict nonclaims.
Only the selected clock feature plus exact runtime marker and explicit caller configuration opens UDP and clock reads.
A bounded exchange batch composes unchanged M evaluation, N first-minimum selection, O offset construction/application, and raw-plus-ClockCorrected sample timestamp retention.
Zero/oversized count, malformed/mismatched/non-finite response, timeout, cancellation, and immediate UDP port reuse return typed bounded outcomes.
No periodic scheduler, drift/dejitter/smoothing, queue/recovery, endpoint selection, devices, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit evidence, closed activation, integrated runtime, tests, and routing coherently before Standard validation; keep lifecycle and push commits intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route integrated bounded clock acquisition/selection/mapping and retain scheduler/authority exclusions.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run owner, clock-loopback, public-boundary, lock, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Describe explicit clock correction without periodic/currentness claims.
Document clock ownership, finite exchange batch, selection, mapping, cancellation, and cleanup.
Separate framing observation from independent integrated runtime claims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused evidence and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bind official endpoint and private clock-probe hashes without raw publication.
Route exchange, selection, mapping, timeout, cancellation, malformed-peer, and cleanup checks.
Review public/private and direct-network routing; no shared mutation expected.
Review clock/data-plane ownership and closed activation; no shared mutation expected.

non_scope: Implicit default count, periodic scheduling, history, drift fitting, smoothing, dejitter, regular-rate scheduling, queues, backpressure, retry, recovery, background work, async runtime, or persistent sockets.
Endpoint discovery/selection, non-loopback reachability, performance/currentness, asymmetric-delay correction, ecosystem-wide compatibility, authentication, or authorization.

Official implementation source, copied or translated source, dependencies, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, inbound-sample commands, or Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Integrate one dependency-free caller-configured UDP clock runtime with the accepted arithmetic spine: acquire an explicit bounded nonzero batch of protocol-110 four-timestamp exchanges, evaluate each finite exchange, select the first minimum-RTT result, construct its finite offset, and explicitly map one accepted raw sample timestamp to ClockCorrected while retaining the raw timestamp separately.
outputs: Sanitized request/response clock framing evidence with private raw artifacts bound by SHA-256.
One selected bounded clock-correction runtime feature with caller-selected bind/peer/count/time bounds and explicit cancellation.
Loopback tests composing acquisition, formula evaluation, minimum-RTT selection, offset construction, and raw-plus-corrected sample timestamp mapping.
